questions.question: If A can do a piece of work in 10 days and B can do the same work in 15 days, how many days will they take to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: Total work = LCM(10, 15) = 30 units. A's efficiency = 3 units/day, B's efficiency = 2 units/day. Combined efficiency = 5 units/day. Time = 30/5 = 6 days.

questions.question: A is twice as efficient as B. If B takes 20 days to finish a task, how many days will A take?
questions.explanation: Since A is twice as efficient, A will take half the time B takes. Time taken by A = 20 / 2 = 10 days.

questions.question: If 10 men can complete a piece of work in 12 days, how many days will 15 men take to complete the same work?
A, B and C can complete a work in 2, 4 and 6 hours respectively. In how many hours will they finish it together?
A can do a work in 15 days. He works for 5 days and then B finishes the remaining work in 20 days. In how many days can B alone do the whole work?
questions.explanation: A's 5 days work = 5/15 = 1/3. Remaining work = 2/3. B takes 20 days for 2/3 work. Full work for B = 20 * (3/2) = 30 days.

questions.question: If A can do a job in 6 days and B can do the same job in 9 days, what fraction of the job can they do in one day together?
questions.explanation: One day's work together = 1/6 + 1/9 = (3+2)/18 = 5/18.

questions.question: A man can do a piece of work in 5 days, but with the help of his son, he can do it in 3 days. In what time can the son do it alone?
questions.explanation: Man's rate = 1/5. Combined rate = 1/3. Son's rate = 1/3 - 1/5 = (5-3)/15 = 2/15. Son's time = 15/2 = 7.5 days.

questions.question: 12 workers can build a wall in 9 days. If the wall needs to be built in 6 days, how many extra workers are needed?
questions.explanation: Total man-days = 12 * 9 = 108. For 6 days, workers needed = 108 / 6 = 18. Extra workers = 18 - 12 = 6.

questions.question: Pipe A can fill a tank in 10 hours and Pipe B can empty it in 15 hours. If both are opened together, in how many hours will the tank be full?
A and B together can do a piece of work in 8 days. If A alone can do it in 12 days, in how many days can B alone do it?
questions.explanation: B's rate = Combined rate - A's rate = 1/8 - 1/12 = (3-2)/24 = 1/24. B's time = 24 days.

questions.question: A can finish a work in 18 days and B can do the same work in half the time taken by A. Then, working together, what part of the same work they can finish in a day?
questions.explanation: A takes 18 days, B takes 9 days. One day's work together = 1/18 + 1/9 = (1+2)/18 = 3/18 = 1/6.

questions.question: If 5 persons can weave 5 carpets in 5 days, then how many carpets will 10 persons weave in 10 days?
A and B can do a work in 12 days, B and C in 15 days, C and A in 20 days. If A, B and C work together, they will complete the work in:
A is 30% more efficient than B. How much time will they, working together, take to complete a job which A alone could have done in 23 days?
questions.explanation: Ratio of efficiency A:B = 130:100 = 13:10. Total work = 13 * 23. Combined efficiency = 13 + 10 = 23. Time = (13 * 23) / 23 = 13 days.

questions.question: One pipe can fill a tank three times as fast as another pipe. If together the two pipes can fill the tank in 36 minutes, then the slower pipe alone will be able to fill the tank in:
questions.explanation: Slower rate = x, Faster rate = 3x. Combined = 4x. 4x = 1/36. x = 1/(36*4) = 1/144. Time = 144 min.

questions.question: A does 80% of a work in 20 days. He then calls in B and they together finish the remaining work in 3 days. How long B alone would take to do the whole work?
questions.explanation: A's full time = 20 / 0.8 = 25 days. Remaining work = 20%. Rate(A+B) = 20% / 3 = 1/15. Rate(B) = Rate(A+B) - Rate(A) = 1/15 - 1/25 = (5-3)/75 = 2/75. Time(B) = 75/2 = 37.5 days.

questions.question: If 7 spiders make 7 webs in 7 days, then 1 spider will make 1 web in how many days?
A can do a piece of work in 4 hours; B and C together can do it in 3 hours, while A and C together can do it in 2 hours. How long will B alone take to do it?
A can complete a work in 12 days. B is 60% more efficient than A. The number of days it takes B to finish the same work is:
questions.explanation: Efficiency B:A = 1.6:1. Time is inversely proportional to efficiency. Time(B) = Time(A) / 1.6 = 12 / 1.6 = 7.5 days.

questions.question: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 and 30 minutes respectively. If both the pipes are opened simultaneously, the tank will be filled in:
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/20 + 1/30 = (3+2)/60 = 5/60 = 1/12. Time = 12 minutes.
